title: Expanded Connect-Board Columns: View More Board Data in Connected Columns
excerpt: When connecting a column to another board, currently only the names of the items are displayed in the connected column. However, users often need access to more detailed information from the connected board within the co…

posts.author: giotor (Giovanne Torres)
posts.cookedHtml: When connecting a column to another board, currently only the names of the items are displayed in the connected column. However, users often need access to more detailed information from the connected board within the connected column. The proposed improvement suggests enhancing the connectivity feature by allowing users to view additional fields or columns from the connected board directly within the connected column. This enhancement would provide users with more comprehensive information without needing to navigate away from the main board view.

posts.author: Prajwal (Prajwal Kumar)
posts.cookedHtml: @giotor Can’t you use Mirror column for that? or you talking about different scenario/use case!

posts.author: giotor (Giovanne Torres)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not really! I want to see more data while I’m selecting, on the selection windown.

Imagine one or more itens with the same name. I wouldn’t know the difference, between them, with just the name. I wish I could see more data from the board connected, to help me to choose the item I want.

image552×663 25.2 KB

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @giotor

Amber here from the monday.com team

Do you find that clicking on the item to bring up the item and more information is a bit too tedious?

Could you please try and explain your use case and shed light on why your items might all be named the same? And what the columns are that would then differentiate these from one another?

I’d love to hear about what your different boards represent and why you’d like to connect them in the first place?

posts.author: Semioticom (David Egert)
posts.cookedHtml:
I totally feel @giotor

To give an example: In our acccounting setup, the Item names (E.g.: Client Invoice Numbers) are extremely cryptic. This means, that I actually have to have the linked boards open, in order to be able to find the information that i need to determine which Item Name is the one I need to choose when linking.
Clicking on the item is not enough, as I have countless items with cryptic names and no pattern to them. So currently i would have to click at random untill I coincidentally find the right item.

posts.author: Mirco0 (Mirco Dalla Lana)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same request, did you solve this issue in the meantime?

posts.author: SwenDekker (Swen Dekker)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 to this request!

We have quite a few items with similar or exactly matching names and mostly differentiate them from eachother using a certain number/text value (can start with 0, so usually written down as text value to keep that intact).
I have been getting a lot of requests to be able to search through the list of items using that number/textvalue instead of the name.

Opening items to see the information is possible, yes, but when you click away, the “Choose items” window is closed, so you have to search again and remember which one you last opened (and hope the list order did not change).
I would not say that is a very user-friendly way to deal with that.

Yes, it is possible to use this method to see more information about the items, but remembering which one you clicked (when the names match exactly) is very prone to (user)errors, which is especially unwanted when connecting items.
It would be nice if it at the very least kept the “Choose items” window open, so you can at least open the found items again and double check (or check another item straight away).

posts.author: SwenDekker (Swen Dekker)
posts.cookedHtml: Update to this problem, our partner was able to mark a column as a CRM-board column and set it up so that you can see certain column values from the connected item (like it’s item-ID for example). This is comparable to how the default CRM setup has connected columns that look different from other connected columns that you can add yourself. Sadly, this option is not available to regular admins right now. Hope we can manage this ourselves in the future!

posts.author: cbieda (Craig Bieda)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also very interested in this feature, we’d have a few boards that would benefit. The main one is when we’re connecting boards to our ‘Project List’ board. The projects have cryptic numbers, it would be great if we could display an additional column from that board in our search window when selecting it. We’d likely show the Customer name in that case.

I like how the search window does show the group names, and that helps us out with making some connections, but for others our group names don’t help much with the selection.
